'''''Tales of the Bounty Hunters''''' is a 1996 short story collection edited by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] featuring five short stories about the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] hunting [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. It is the third installment in the [[Tales series|''Tales'' series]] about minor characters from the [[original trilogy]].

{{Book-stub}}

==Publisher's summary==
''In a wild and battle-scarred galaxy, assassins, pirates, smugglers, and cutthroats of every description roam at will, fearing only the professional bounty hunters—amoral adventurers who track down the scum of the universe…for a fee. When Darth Vader seeks to strike at the heart of the Rebellion by targeting Han Solo and the ''Millennium Falcon'', he calls upon six of the most successful—and feared—hunters, including the merciless Boba Fett. They all have two things in common: lust for profit and contempt for life.…''

''Featuring original stories by Kevin J. Anderson, M. Shayne Bell, Daniel Keys Moran, Kathy Tyers and Dave Wolverton.''

==Continuity==
*The quote attributed to Admiral [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Piett]] at the beginning of the book reads, "Bounty hunters, we don't need ''that'' scum"; he says "Bounty hunters, we don't need ''their'' scum" in the film version of ''The Empire Strikes Back''. The same is true when the quote is repeated on pages 46, 101 and 239.
*On pages 151 and 175, [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk's]] [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] is incorrectly described as orange when, really, it was yellow.
*[[FX-7/Legends|FX-7]] is incorrectly referred to as "Effour-Seven" all but once in "[[Of Possible Futures: The Tale of Zuckuss and 4-LOM]]."
